Add launcher script and improve branch handling in work-issue skill

commit.message:
feat: v3 executive coach pivot + stories 2.8 & 2.9 (#34)

* Implement v3 executive coach pivot — full sprint

- DB: migration 015 (sessions, action_items, solis_queries, usage_tracking, subscriptions; alter clients: add goal/start_date, drop email/phone/linkedin/tags)
- Schema: update client.ts (v3 fields); add session, actionItem, subscription schemas
- AI abstraction: provider interface + Claude/OpenAI/placeholder impls; prompts, summarize, solis RAG, embeddings
- Transcription abstraction: Deepgram Nova-2 + placeholder
- Billing abstraction: Stripe + placeholder; checkUsage quota enforcement
- API: sessions CRUD + summarize; intelligence/query (hybrid RAG); stripe checkout/webhook/portal; action-items CRUD; subscriptions/status
- Client API: free limit 3→1 via subscriptions table
- UI: ClientForm (goal/start_date, rm phone/email/linkedin/tags); ClientCard (goal, last session, pending actions); ClientSearch (rm tags, last-session sort)
- New components: SessionTimeline, SessionCard, SessionUploadModal, ActionItemList, SolisPanel
- New pages: clients/[id] (client detail), intelligence, settings (billing)
- Dashboard: LeftSidebar nav (Clients, Intelligence, Settings); update layout
- Config: add AI_PROVIDER, TRANSCRIPTION_PROVIDER, BILLING_PROVIDER, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, DEEPGRAM_API_KEY, Stripe vars

Fix invalid date in knowledge tab for negative UTC offsets

normalizeTimestamp() only checked for + and Z timezone indicators,
missing negative offsets like -03:00. This caused it to append Z to
already-valid ISO timestamps, producing unparseable dates.

commit.message:
feat: phases 7-9 — 34 new challenges, 18 docs, 4 course modules

Phase 7 — Challenge Expansion:
- CH-061 to CH-068: Feature validation (search, browse, sync APIs)
- CH-069 to CH-075: Security validation (headers, CORS, injection, JWT)
- CH-076 to CH-083: Performance & resilience (latency, concurrency, WebSocket)
- CH-084 to CH-088: Monitoring (Prometheus, HTTP metrics, Grafana)
- MOD-016 to MOD-021: Module functional verification (Lazy, Recovery, Memory)
- Total challenges: ~285 (up from ~249)

Phase 8 — Documentation:
- 3 API reference docs (Search, Browse, Sync)
- 3 security docs (Headers, CORS, Secrets)
- 2 architecture docs (Lazy Loading, Concurrency Control)
- 2 guides (Performance Tuning, Stress Test Results)
- 4 CLAUDE.md files (api-client, desktop, wizard, website)

Phase 9 — Course Content:
- Module 13 script: Search, Browse & Cloud Sync
- Module 14 script: Challenge System Deep Dive
- Module 9 slides: Search & Sync
- Module 10 slides: Advanced Testing
- Updated course outline with new modules

commit.message:
fix(ego-lint): correct R-SLOP-01 provenance post-filter matching (#85)

## Summary

- **Fix PASS_COUNT double increment** in provenance post-filter:
`print_result("PASS", ...)` already increments PASS_COUNT via its case
statement, so the manual `PASS_COUNT += 1` on the line before was
double-counting
- **Fix parse-lint-results.py provenance regex**: `score\s+(\d+)` didn't
match the actual output format `provenance-score=4` — changed to
`provenance-score=(\d+)`. This caused `provenance_score: null` in all
field test baseline JSON files
- **Add test fixture** `provenance-jsdoc@test` with JSDoc patterns +
high provenance signals (score=4) to verify the post-filter correctly
suppresses R-SLOP-01/02 WARNs
- **Update dash-to-panel annotation** from `fp` to `expected` — the
stdout suppression has been working since the deferred approach
(526a868), the annotation was stale

## Test plan

- [x] `bash tests/run-tests.sh` — 608 passed, 0 failed
- [x] `provenance-jsdoc@test` fixture verifies: suppression PASS line
present, no R-SLOP-01/02 WARNs in output, provenance score extracted
correctly
- [x] `ai-slop@test` still shows R-SLOP-01 WARNs (low provenance, not
suppressed)
- [x] Python unit test `test_provenance_score` updated to use real
output format

Closes #73

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Full Script parser now runs BEFORE AI slides (was being overridden)

Root cause: fetchAISlides (Supabase Edge Function) was called first,
returning 2 slides from value_text. This overrode the Full Script
parser which correctly detected 9 sections.

Priority order now: Full Script parser > AI slides > value_text fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: address all code review findings

[sev 2] bot: guard against None affiliate_url/source in result formatter
[sev 2] bot: lower httpx timeout to 70s (Claude service times out at 60s)
[sev 2] docker-compose: add backend healthcheck; dependents use service_healthy
[sev 1] bot: merge pre-selected notes with typed free-text instead of discarding
[sev 1] builder: drop failed build from in-memory store before raising 502
[sev 1] models: remove min_length=3 on notes field (rejects valid short input)
[sev 1] nginx script: replace fragile container name grep with curl health checks
[sev 1] nginx script: add --max-time to final curl health check

space is always occupied from the very first render.

perf: fused GeGLU+NVFP4 GEMV+residual kernel, multi-row GeGLU dispatch

models using GELU-tanh, mirroring the SwiGLU fusion pattern.
Includes multi-row variant for small K dispatch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Kimi K2.5 crashing after first tool call in thinking mode

handle_stream discarded reasoning_content when returning tool calls,
so the reconstructed ChatCompletionMessage was missing it. Kimi's API
requires reasoning_content in assistant messages when thinking is
enabled, causing "reasoning_content is missing" 400 errors on every
multi-tool interaction.

Now handle_stream returns reasoning alongside tool data, and
process_prompt passes it through to the message object.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(config): remove microphone=() from Permissions-Policy — was blocking dictate

The Permissions-Policy header was set to block microphone on all pages:
  camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()

This caused the browser to refuse mic access at the HTTP header level before
any JS ran, making SpeechRecognition immediately fire not-allowed regardless
of what the user permitted in browser settings.

Removed microphone=() so the browser's normal site-level permission prompt
can appear when the user clicks Dictate.

commit.message:
chore(infra): rename dev-vps to infisical-vps in inventory (CAB-1751) (#1572)

## Summary
- Rename `dev-vps` to `infisical-vps` in `vps-inventory.sh` after
Infisical migration from Hetzner K3s to OVH VPS

## Context
Infisical was migrated from Hetzner K3s (`46.225.38.168`) to OVH VPS
(`213.199.45.108`) with:
- Docker Compose (Infisical + PostgreSQL 15 + Redis 7) + Caddy TLS
- DNS switched: `vault.gostoa.dev` + `staging-vault.gostoa.dev`
- 8/8 binary DoD passed, 5/5 HEGEMON workers verified

## Test plan
- [x] All HEGEMON workers auth + secret load verified
- [x] bench-vps + n8n-vps connectivity verified
- [x] `infisical-token` helper works end-to-end

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
